title: 10 best herbal teas for colds
intro_desc: Herbal teas for colds are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate the symptoms of a common cold. These teas can soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and promote relaxation, making it easier for individuals to rest and recover. Examples of herbal teas that help with colds include chamomile, peppermint, ginger, and echinacea. For instance, chamomile tea can help promote sleep and soothe a sore throat, while ginger tea can reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion. By drinking these teas, individuals can find relief from their symptoms and improve their overall quality of life during a cold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with colds because it has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate symptoms and potentially shorten the duration of a cold. The herb contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins that work together to reduce inflammation, soothe a sore throat, and relieve congestion. Additionally, yarrow promotes perspiration, which can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever. Its immune-boosting properties make it an ideal natural remedy for preventing and treating colds. The warm tea can also provide comfort and relief by soothing a scratchy throat and providing hydration during illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and making it easier to breathe. This is important for colds as inflammation can exacerbate cold symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antipyretic: Yarrow tea can help reduce fever associated with colds, providing relief and promoting recovery. This property is important for colds as fever can cause discomfort and dehydration. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids and volatile oils.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause colds. This property is important for colds as it can help shorten the duration of the illness and prevent the spread of the cold to others. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, flavonoids, and volatile oils.

Diaphoretic: Yarrow tea can promote sweating, which helps the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever. This property is important for colds as it can help the body eliminate the virus and recover more quickly. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.

Expectorant: Yarrow tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion. This property is important for colds as it can help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Place the measured yarrow in a tea infuser or teapot and pour the hot water over it.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea, then drink it while it's still warm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, yarrow flowers help reduce inflammation and boost the immune system.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, yarrow leaves are often used to treat cold symptoms like congestion and cough.
Stems: High in flavonoids, yarrow stems can help alleviate symptoms of colds by promoting perspiration and reducing fever.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are known for their carminative properties, helping to soothe digestive issues often associated with colds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with colds because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when it comes into contact with water. This mucilage coats and soothes the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation. Additionally, marshmallow tea is known to have expectorant properties, which help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract. The herb also has anti-tussive properties, which can help to suppress a persistent cough. Furthermore, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms. Drinking marshmallow tea can provide relief from a sore throat, cough, and congestion, making it an excellent natural remedy for colds.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tea is rich in mucilage, which forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, thus alleviating cold symptoms.
Anti-tussive: The tea's mild sedative effect helps suppress coughing, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits, which are common during colds.

Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, easing congestion and discomfort caused by colds.
Antimicrobial: The tea's polysaccharides have antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against secondary bacterial infections that can occur during a cold.

Expectorant: Marshmallow tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and clear airways, thus improving breathing and reducing cold-related congestion.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 quart of water, 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of dried elder flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Serve the tea hot, optionally adding honey or lemon to taste.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamin C and anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow leaves help alleviate cold symptoms.
Flowers: Containing antioxidants and mucilage, marshmallow flowers soothe sore throats and boost the immune system during colds.
Roots: High in mucilage, marshmallow roots help relieve irritation, inflammation, and congestion in the respiratory tract.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients, marshmallow seeds provide mild diuretic effects, supporting overall health during cold recovery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with colds because it contains aromatic compounds, such as camphor and thujone, which can help to relieve congestion and open up the airways. Additionally, wormwood has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and fight off infection. Furthermore, wormwood tea can help to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which are essential for fighting off viruses and bacteria. The warm nature of the tea can also help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate coughing. Drinking wormwood tea can provide relief from cold symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tea can help inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, which are common causes of colds, due to the presence of active constituents such as camphene, cineole, and thujone.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood tea can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of colds, thanks to the presence of compounds like beta-myrcene and limonene.

Expectorant properties: Wormwood tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion, due to the action of compounds such as 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.

Fever-reducing properties: Wormwood tea can help lower fever associated with colds by promoting perspiration and cooling the body, partly due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones like artabsin and artemisinin.

Immune-boosting properties: Wormwood tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off colds and other infections, due to the presence of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried wormwood herb to a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the hot water over the wormwood herb and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strainer and discard the used herb.
Sweeten the tea with honey or lemon, if desired, and drink up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood leaves are rich in phenolic compounds and flavonoids, making them effective in alleviating cold symptoms.
Flowers: Wormwood flowers have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce cold-related inflammation.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds are a natural source of antioxidants and essential oils, aiding in soothing cold-related discomforts.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with colds because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, cinnamon has been shown to boost the immune system, helping the body fight off viruses and infections more effectively. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to alleviate congestion and clear up stuffy noses. Furthermore, cinnamon tea is a natural source of antimicrobial agents, which can help to kill off harmful bacteria and viruses in the body. Overall, drinking cinnamon tea can provide relief from cold symptoms and support the body's natural defenses against illness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Cinnamon tea can help fight off harmful bacteria and viruses that can cause colds, due to the presence of compounds such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon tea can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of a cold, thanks to the presence of compounds such as cinnamaldehyde and flavonoids.
Immune-boosting properties: Cinnamon tea can help strengthen the immune system and prevent the onset of colds, due to the presence of antioxidants such as polyphenols and proanthocyanidins.

Antioxidant properties: Cinnamon tea can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can weaken the immune system and make the body more susceptible to colds, due to the presence of antioxidants such as polyphenols and proanthocyanidins.
Decongestant properties: Cinnamon tea can help clear nasal congestion and make it easier to breathe, due to the presence of compounds such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 cinnamon stick and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, then add 1 teaspoon of dried herbal tea (such as chamomile, elderberry, or echinacea) and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea, then add honey and lemon to taste.
Serve hot and enjoy.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tree is commonly used in medicinal teas for colds due to its anti-inflammatory and warming properties.
Seeds: Cinnamon seeds are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, making them a valuable addition to teas for treating colds.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon plant have antimicrobial and expectorant properties, making them useful for treating respiratory symptoms associated with colds.
Stems: The stems, or rhizomes, of cinnamon contain high levels of cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to have antiviral and immune-boosting effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with colds because it is made from a blend of herbs that have natural healing properties. The tea contains echinacea, which is known to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. It also has licorice root, which soothes a sore throat and relieves congestion. Peppermint leaf is another ingredient that helps to break up mucus and relieve coughing. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion. Drinking Eureka tea can provide relief from cold symptoms and help the body to fight off the virus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Eureka tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect the body's cells from damage caused by free radicals, boosting the immune system and aiding in cold recovery. Active constituents include flavonoids and catechins.

Antimicrobial properties: Eureka tea has antimicrobial properties, which help in preventing the growth of bacteria and viruses, thus reducing the risk of infections and the severity of cold symptoms. The active constituents responsible for these properties are primarily essential oils and alkaloids.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of Eureka tea help alleviate symptoms associated with colds, such as sore throat, runny nose, and congestion. Active constituents include polyphenols and caffeine.
Vitamin C content: Eureka tea is rich in Vitamin C, which supports the immune system and helps the body fight off infections, making it an essential component in managing colds.

Decongestant properties: Eureka tea contains compounds that act as natural decongestants, helping to clear blocked nasal passages and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief for cold symptoms. The active constituents responsible for these properties are primarily essential oils and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried elderberry flowers, 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, and 1 quart of water; bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the herbs simmer for 15 minutes to extract their properties.
Remove from heat, then add 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea root and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid herbs, then stir in honey and lemon juice to taste (optional).
Serve the herbal Eureka tea hot, and enjoy its soothing and potentially immune-boosting effects during cold season.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for colds due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids, eureka plant flowers are used in teas for colds because of their antimicrobial and antiviral effects.
Seeds: A great source of fatty acids, eureka plant seeds are included in medicinal teas for colds to provide additional nutritional support and immune system boost.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with colds because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds, such as sore throat, cough, and congestion. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause colds. Furthermore, it can also help soothe a sore throat and alleviate a fever. Drinking eastern purple coneflower tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage cold symptoms and support overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Eastern purple coneflower tea can help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses, providing relief from cold symptoms. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant properties: The tea can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are often associated with cold symptoms. This is attributed to the high levels of antioxidants, including chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.

Immune-boosting properties: Eastern purple coneflower tea can help enhance the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off cold viruses. This is due to the presence of active compounds like alkamides and polyacetylenes, which have been shown to stimulate the immune response.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea can help alleviate inflammation and reduce symptoms such as sore throat and congestion. This is due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds, including phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Diaphoretic properties: Eastern purple coneflower tea can help promote sweating, which can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers and add to a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes to reach a temperature of 200-212°F.
Pour the hot water over the coneflower flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strainer and discard the spent flowers.
Drink the tea immediately while it's still warm, up to three times a day for cold relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in medicinal compounds, the flowers of eastern purple coneflower are used in teas to help alleviate cold symptoms.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, coneflower leaves can help boost the immune system and provide relief from cold-related issues.
Roots: Packed with medicinal properties, coneflower roots can be used in teas to soothe cold symptoms and promote recovery.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and nutrients, coneflower seeds can be used in teas to help reduce cold-related inflammation and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tea helps with colds because it contains powerful antiviral and immune-boosting properties. The herb, also known as Eleutherococcus senticosus, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including colds and flu. The active compounds in Siberian ginseng, such as eleutherosides, help to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and antibodies that fight off viruses. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as sore throat, cough, and congestion. Drinking Siberian ginseng tea can provide relief from cold symptoms and help to speed up recovery time.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Siberian ginseng tea helps the body adapt to physical, chemical, and biological stressors, which is crucial during cold infections as it supports the immune system and promotes overall well-being. Active constituents include eleutherosides and polysaccharides.

Immune system enhancement: Siberian ginseng tea boosts the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off cold viruses. The active compounds responsible are eleutherosides, phenylpropanoids, and saponins.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of Siberian ginseng tea help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate cold symptoms. The antioxidants responsible are eleutherosides, polyphenols, and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory property: Siberian ginseng tea has anti-inflammatory effects, which can alleviate symptoms of a cold, such as a sore throat and congestion. The active constituents responsible are eleutherosides, saponins, and polysaccharides.

Energy-boosting property: Siberian ginseng tea can increase energy levels and reduce fatigue, which is important during a cold as it helps the body recover faster. The active compounds responsible are eleutherosides, ginsenosides, and polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 4 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Siberian ginseng root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink while warm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Siberian ginseng are commonly used in medicinal teas for colds due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to better cope with stress and boost the immune system.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Siberian ginseng contain beneficial compounds, such as eleutherosides, which have been shown to enhance immune function and reduce symptoms of colds.

Leaves: The leaves of Siberian ginseng are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing symptoms of colds and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Seeds: The seeds of Siberian ginseng contain high levels of eleutherosides, which can help to strengthen the immune system and reduce the severity and duration of colds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with colds because it contains an abundance of antioxidants, which can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The herb is also known for its diuretic properties, which can help eliminate toxins from the body and reduce congestion. Additionally, horsetail tea contains silica, a mineral that can help strengthen the immune system and promote the healing of damaged tissues. Furthermore, the tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause colds. Drinking horsetail tea can provide relief from cold symptoms such as a runny nose, sore throat, and cough.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the bacteria and viruses that cause colds. This is important because it can help reduce the duration and severity of a cold. The active constituents that give horsetail its antimicrobial properties include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. This is important because inflammation can exacerbate cold symptoms and make it difficult to breathe. The active constituents that give horsetail its anti-inflammatory properties include saponins and tannins.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can weaken the immune system and make a person more susceptible to colds. This is important because a strong immune system is better able to fight off viruses and bacteria that cause colds. The active constituents that give horsetail its antioxidant properties include phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Diuretic: Horsetail tea has diuretic properties that can help flush toxins out of the body, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms. This is important because toxins can exacerbate cold symptoms and make a person feel worse. The active constituents that give horsetail its diuretic properties include saponins and flavonoids.

Expectorant: Horsetail tea has expectorant properties that can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. This is important because mucus can make it difficult to breathe and exacerbate cold symptoms. The active constituents that give horsetail its expectorant properties include saponins and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb (Equisetum arvense).
Steep the horsetail in hot water for 5-10 minutes, covered.
Strain the tea to remove the herb.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with colds because it contains antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms and reduce the duration of a cold. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia and has been used for centuries by indigenous people for its medicinal benefits. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptus oil and tannins, can help soothe a sore throat, relieve congestion, and reduce fever. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants can help boost the immune system and protect against further infections. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage cold symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains compounds such as eucalyptol and terpinen-4-ol, which have antimicrobial effects and can help to combat the bacteria and viruses that cause colds.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms such as congestion and sore throat.

Expectorant properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains compounds such as alpha-pinene and limonene, which have expectorant properties and can help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Analgesic properties: The tea has analgesic properties, which can help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with colds, such as headaches and muscle aches.
Immune-boosting properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains antioxidants and other compounds that can help to boost the immune system and improve the body's ability to fight off infections.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of fresh Tasmanian blue gum leaves with 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove leaves, pouring it into a teapot or individual cups.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve the tea hot and enjoy for soothing relief of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for colds due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum plant is used in teas for its antimicrobial and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to boost the immune system and provide relief from cold symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used in teas for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are rich in essential oils and have been used in teas for their expectorant and decongestant properties, which can help clear blocked airways.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with colds because it has antiviral and expectorant properties that can soothe a sore throat and reduce phlegm. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including the rhinovirus, which is a common cause of the common cold. Additionally, licorice tea can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from symptoms such as coughing and congestion. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to alleviate sinus pressure and promote relaxation, making it easier to rest and recover from a cold.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Licorice tea helps loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and expel from the respiratory tract, which is important for relieving congestion and irritation in the airways caused by colds. This property is attributed to the presence of glycyrrhizin and other saponins in licorice root.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as sore throat and irritation, which are common during colds. The active constituents responsible for this effect are flavonoids, chalcones, and isoflavans.

Antiviral: Licorice tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help the body fight off the rhinovirus, the primary cause of the common cold. The triterpenoid glycyrrhizin is the main active constituent responsible for this antiviral effect.

Immune system support: Licorice tea can help boost the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off infections and reducing the severity and duration of colds. The polysaccharides found in licorice root are believed to contribute to this immune-enhancing effect.

Soothing: Licorice tea has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes of the respiratory tract, providing relief from symptoms such as coughing, sore throat, and irritation. The demulcent properties of licorice root, which form a protective film over mucous membranes, are responsible for this soothing effect.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 8 ounces of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain the liquid to remove the licorice root.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve the tea hot and enjoy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for colds due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and relieve congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant are used in teas for their ability to stimulate the immune system, providing relief from cold symptoms.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are often included in medicinal teas for colds because of their antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which can help to shorten the duration of a cold.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant is used in teas for its soothing effects on the respiratory system, making it helpful in treating cold-related coughs and congestion.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat colds with herbal teas is by choosing teas with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, such as echinacea, ginger, and elderberry. These teas can help soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and boost the immune system to fight off the cold virus. Drinking warm tea can also help alleviate symptoms by increasing hydration and relieving nasal congestion. For best results, drink at least 2-3 cups of herbal tea daily at the first sign of a cold. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can help manage cold symptoms, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to colds that are treated with herbal teas include sore throats, coughs, and congestion. Peppermint tea can soothe a sore throat and act as a natural decongestant. Ginger tea is known to alleviate nausea and reduce inflammation, often caused by a common cold. Chamomile tea has antimicrobial properties and can help with relaxation, promoting better sleep. Licorice root tea can also soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation, while thyme tea has been shown to loosen mucus and relieve coughs. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any herbal tea regimen, especially for pregnant women, children, and individuals with underlying health conditions.
